hero.headline: Amrita Online MBA in Operations Management: Syllabus, Fees and Career Outcomes
hero.sub: NAAC A++ | NIRF #8 University 2025 | UGC-DEB approved | WES recognised

sections.tldr: Amrita Online MBA in Operations Management covers business forecasting, ERP, operations research, advanced supply chain management, and TQM. Total tuition Rs 2,24,000 (semester fee Rs 56,000). Plus Rs 700 registration and Rs 11,000 exam fees. Total cost of programme Rs 2,35,700. Zero-cost EMI available directly through the university. NAAC A++. NIRF #8 University 2025. WES recognised for USA and Canada.

about.body:
Operations Management is the backbone specialisation for manufacturing, logistics, supply chain, and process improvement careers in India's growing industrial and services sectors. Amrita Vishwa Vidyapeetham Online's MBA in Operations Management covers the full stack - from business forecasting and ERP implementation through operations research, advanced supply chain, and TQM - equipping graduates to manage complex operational systems at scale.

The Sem 3-4 curriculum begins with the quantitative and systems foundations of business forecasting and ERP in Semester 3, then advances to operations research, supply chain optimisation, and quality management in Semester 4. This sequencing prepares graduates for both manufacturing plant management and technology-enabled supply chain roles.

Amrita holds NIRF #8 University 2025 and NAAC A++ - India's highest accreditation grade. WES recognition gives graduates formal degree equivalence in the USA and Canada, relevant for candidates targeting global supply chain or operations roles with multinational manufacturers. The Study Abroad option includes exchanges at Oakland University (USA), University of New Mexico (USA), Institut Mines Telecom (France), and University of Barcelona (Spain).

EdifyEdu verifies data independently and takes no commission from any university.

syllabus.note: Sem 1-2 subjects are shared across all Amrita MBA specialisations. Sem 3-4 below are specific to the Operations Management specialisation.

subjects.name: International Business
subjects.desc: Global trade frameworks, cross-border market entry strategies, FDI analysis, and managing international operations.

subjects.name: Business Analytics and Business Modelling
subjects.desc: Quantitative business modelling, regression analysis, forecasting techniques, and data-driven decision-making frameworks.

subjects.name: Business Forecasting
subjects.desc: Demand forecasting methods, time series analysis, regression-based forecasting, and forecast accuracy measurement for operations planning.

subjects.name: ERP
subjects.desc: ERP implementation lifecycle, SAP and Oracle ERP module overview, change management for ERP rollouts, and ERP ROI assessment.

subjects.name: Education for Life
subjects.desc: Soft skills development, mindfulness, health and wellbeing, and holistic personal effectiveness for professionals.

subjects.name: Environment Sustainability and Governance
subjects.desc: ESG reporting frameworks, corporate sustainability strategy, carbon accounting basics, and governance obligations.

subjects.name: Project
subjects.desc: Applied industry operations project integrating specialisation knowledge with real-world process optimisation or supply chain problem-solving.

subjects.name: Operations Research
subjects.desc: Linear programming, transportation models, assignment models, queuing theory basics, and simulation techniques for operations decision-making.

subjects.name: Advanced Supply Chain Management
subjects.desc: Global supply chain design, supplier risk management, inventory optimisation models, S&OP integration, and supply chain resilience frameworks.

subjects.name: TQM
subjects.desc: Total Quality Management principles, Six Sigma DMAIC methodology, statistical process control, ISO 9001 quality management system, and lean manufacturing basics.

whoHires.body:
Operations Management MBA graduates from Amrita Vishwa Vidyapeetham Online are hired across India's manufacturing, logistics, and supply chain sector:

**Industries:** Automotive manufacturing, FMCG supply chain, e-commerce logistics, pharma manufacturing, steel and metals, IT services delivery operations, and consulting (operations practice)

**Entry-level roles:** Operations Executive, Supply Chain Analyst, Production Supervisor (management trainee track), ERP Business Analyst, Procurement Analyst, Quality Analyst

**Mid-level roles (3-5 years experience):** Operations Manager, Supply Chain Manager, Plant Manager (mid-scale), ERP Project Manager, Procurement Manager, Quality Manager

**Senior roles (7+ years):** VP Operations, Supply Chain Director, Head of Manufacturing, Chief Operating Officer (mid-market), VP Procurement, Head of Lean and Quality

**Top hiring organisations:** Tata Motors, Maruti Suzuki, Mahindra Manufacturing, HUL supply chain, ITC supply chain, Flipkart logistics, Amazon India operations, Delhivery, Blue Dart, Dr Reddy's manufacturing, Lupin operations, TCS delivery operations, and operations consulting firms including McKinsey Ops, Deloitte Operations, and KPMG Supply Chain

skills.body:
The Operations Management specialisation builds skills that manufacturing, supply chain, and operations consulting employers test at analyst and manager levels:

**Technical skills:**
- Business Forecasting: exponential smoothing, ARIMA basics, demand sensing, forecast accuracy KPIs (MAPE, MAD), and S&OP process design
- ERP: SAP MM and PP module overview, Oracle SCM basics, ERP implementation project stages, business process mapping for ERP, and user acceptance testing management
- Operations Research: linear programming formulation and solver usage, transportation problem setup, assignment model application, and waiting line model analysis
- TQM and Six Sigma: DMAIC project structure, control chart interpretation, process capability analysis, root cause analysis tools (5-Why, fishbone), and ISO 9001 audit readiness

**Applied skills:**
- Supply Chain Management: inventory policy design (EOQ, safety stock), supplier scorecard design, network optimisation basics, and supply chain risk heat mapping
- Process Improvement: value stream mapping, waste identification in manufacturing and services, lean project framing, and Kaizen event facilitation

**Soft skills:** Leading cross-functional operations improvement projects, communicating process change to plant floor teams, managing vendor relationship and escalations, presenting operations KPIs to board and executive committees, and driving ERP adoption across resistant operational teams.

comparisons.body:
Comparing Amrita Online MBA Operations Management against peer programmes:

**vs Chandigarh University Online MBA Operations Management:** CU at NIRF #19 with PwC co-teaching and Harvard certificate offers a strong operations curriculum at Rs 1,65,000 (Tier 1). CU is compelling for candidates wanting PwC credentials at a lower fee. Amrita at Rs 2,24,000 adds NAAC A++ (vs CU's A+), NIRF #8 (vs #19), and WES recognition - the premium is justified for candidates where institutional credibility and international career value apply.

**vs LPU Online MBA Operations Management:** LPU at NAAC A++ offers an operations management track but without the same NIRF rank as Amrita. For candidates specifically targeting NIRF #8 credentials or WES recognition alongside operations management, Amrita is the clear choice.

**vs Shoolini University Online MBA Operations and Supply Chain Management:** Shoolini at NAAC A and NIRF #92 has a dedicated operations-supply-chain spec. For candidates who require NAAC A++ or a higher NIRF rank for employer credential filters in manufacturing or operations consulting, Amrita is the appropriate upgrade.

fees.body:
The Amrita Vishwa Vidyapeetham Online MBA in Operations Management carries a tuition fee of Rs 2,24,000 total across 4 semesters (2-year programme).

**Mandatory fees (all students, disclose before enrolment):**
- Registration fee: Rs 700 (one-time)
- Examination fee: Rs 2,750 per semester x 4 = Rs 11,000 total
- **Total cost of programme: Rs 2,24,000 + Rs 700 + Rs 11,000 = Rs 2,35,700**

**Payment options for Indian students:**
- Semester fee: Rs 56,000 per semester
- Annual fee with 5% discount: Rs 1,06,400 per year
- Zero-cost EMI: available directly through Amrita Vishwa Vidyapeetham (contact counsellor for current monthly instalment and tenure options)

**Foreign national students:**
Contact our counsellor for foreign national tuition track applicable to the Operations Management specialisation.

Fees listed are indicative. EdifyEdu reconfirms fees with Amrita each quarter. Please reconfirm with our counsellor before any payment.

certificate.body:
Graduates receive an Amrita Vishwa Vidyapeetham MBA degree certificate with Operations Management noted on the marksheet, issued under UGC-DEB 2020 online mode standards.

NAAC A++ and NIRF #8 University 2025 are the strongest institutional credentials in India's online MBA space. WES recognition gives formal degree equivalence in the USA and Canada - relevant for operations professionals targeting global manufacturing companies, MNC supply chain roles, or Canadian PR pathways. DigiLocker verification is available.

reviews.intro: Three verified reviews from Amrita Vishwa Vidyapeetham Online MBA Operations Management alumni. Ratings reflect individual experience.

items.name: Suresh P.
items.liked: Advanced Supply Chain Management subject gave me the S&OP and inventory optimisation frameworks I apply directly in my automotive OEM role
items.disliked: ERP content covered SAP module overview well but hands-on SAP sandbox access would have made it more practically actionable
items.body: I manage supply chain operations at an automotive component manufacturer. The Business Forecasting, Advanced Supply Chain, and TQM subjects were directly applicable to my daily operations work. NAAC A++ from NIRF #8 is the strongest credential available in online MBA space - it cleared the management degree filter at every manufacturing firm I interviewed with. Zero-cost EMI made the Rs 2,24,000 tuition manageable on my existing salary.

items.name: Anand K.
items.liked: Operations Research subject gave me the LP and simulation frameworks that e-commerce logistics planning relies on
items.disliked: TQM subject content was thorough but updated case studies from e-commerce fulfilment centres would make it more relevant for digital operations roles
items.body: I moved from a technology role to an operations management track at an e-commerce logistics company using this MBA. The Operations Research and Advanced Supply Chain subjects were directly applicable. NAAC A++ from NIRF #8 is recognised at every MNC operations employer I engaged with. Study Abroad option was available and I used the Institut Mines Telecom France exchange to build a European supply chain network.

items.name: Lakshmi M.
items.liked: TQM and Six Sigma content gave me the quality management framework to lead our ISO 9001 re-certification project
items.disliked: Business Forecasting content was strong on statistical methods but would benefit from demand sensing and real-time analytics tools integration
items.body: I work in quality management at a pharma manufacturing company. TQM, Business Forecasting, and ERP subjects were directly applicable to my plant operations role. NAAC A++ cleared the education quality filter at every pharma MNC I approached. WES recognition is relevant for my target of a global supply chain role in the US market within 3 years.

faqs.question: Is the Operations Management MBA suitable for manufacturing plant managers?
faqs.answer: Yes. Business Forecasting, ERP, Operations Research, Advanced Supply Chain Management, and TQM are all directly applicable for plant management roles. The curriculum covers quantitative production planning, quality management (Six Sigma, ISO 9001), and ERP implementation alongside supply chain strategy. NAAC A++ and NIRF #8 satisfy the management degree credential filters at automotive, FMCG, and pharma manufacturing employers.

faqs.question: Does the MBA cover Six Sigma and Lean Manufacturing?
faqs.answer: Yes. The TQM subject covers Six Sigma DMAIC methodology, statistical process control, lean manufacturing basics, value stream mapping, and ISO 9001 quality management system. The focus is on practical application - DMAIC project structure, control chart reading, and root cause analysis - rather than full Green Belt certification coursework. For candidates seeking Six Sigma Green Belt or Black Belt certification alongside the MBA, separate specialised programmes are available.

faqs.question: What is the total cost of the Amrita Operations Management MBA including all fees?
faqs.answer: Total cost of programme is Rs 2,35,700 - comprising Rs 2,24,000 tuition, Rs 700 one-time registration, and Rs 11,000 examination fees (Rs 2,750 per semester across 4 semesters). Zero-cost EMI is available directly through Amrita Vishwa Vidyapeetham. Annual fee with 5% discount is Rs 1,06,400 per year.

faqs.question: Does the Operations Management MBA cover SAP ERP in practical depth?
faqs.answer: The ERP subject covers SAP MM and PP module overview, Oracle SCM basics, ERP implementation lifecycle, business process mapping, and change management for ERP adoption. The focus is on ERP project management and business process alignment - not ERP development or configuration programming. For candidates managing ERP implementation projects or ERP-driven operations transformation, this provides the management vocabulary and process framework that project sponsors require.

faqs.question: Is the Operations Management MBA useful for supply chain consulting roles?
faqs.answer: Yes. Operations Research, Advanced Supply Chain Management, Business Forecasting, and TQM together build the analytical and process frameworks that operations consulting firms (McKinsey Ops, Deloitte Operations, KPMG Supply Chain) test at entry and manager level. NAAC A++ and NIRF #8 satisfy management degree quality filters at Big-4 and MBB consulting firms for operations-track applications.

chooseIf: You want an Operations Management MBA from India's only NAAC A++ online university with NIRF #8 ranking.
WES recognition for USA and Canada career applications or global manufacturing roles is a priority.
You want Business Forecasting, ERP, Operations Research, Advanced Supply Chain, and TQM in one curriculum.
Your target employers in manufacturing, logistics, or operations consulting filter by NAAC grade or NIRF rank.
consider: You want PwC co-teaching and Harvard certificate at a lower price point (consider Chandigarh University Operations Management).
You want a combined operations and supply chain spec (consider Shoolini Operations and Supply Chain Management).
Your budget is under Rs 2,00,000 and NAAC A++ is not a filter for your target employers.
